failed_attempts:
the walk, and if we can prove that the graph homomorphism induced by `s = 1` is trivial, then the `map` will do nothing.

But perhaps instead of trying to be

have h₂ : (x.2.map (K n) (permMap' n s)) = x.2 := by
        apply identityWalkMap
        <;> simp [s]
        <;> aesop
      simpa [h₂] using h₂

<;> simp_all [permMapWalk]
      <;> rfl
    · -- Prove symmetry
      intro p q h
      obtain ⟨s, h_eq, h_walk⟩ := h
      have s_inv : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := s.symm
      have h₁ : s_inv q.1 = p.1 := by
        have h₂ : s p.1 = q.1 := h_eq
        have h₃ : s_inv (s p.1) = s_inv q.1 := by rw [h₂]
        have h₄ : s_inv (s p.1) = p.1 := by
          simp [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]
        rw [h₄] at h₃
        exact h₃
      have h₂ : h₁ ▸ (permMapWalk n s_inv q.2) = p.2 := by
        have h₃ : h_eq ▸ (permMapWalk n s p.2) = q.2 := h_walk
        have h₄ : permMapWalk n s_inv (h_eq ▸ (permMapWalk n s p.2)) = permMapWalk n s_inv q.2 := by rw [h₃]
        have h₅ : permMapWalk n s_inv (h_eq ▸ (permMapWalk n s p.2)) = p.2 := by
          simp [permMapWalk, Equiv.Perm.sign_symm, Equiv.Perm.sign_inv] at h₃ ⊢
          <;>
          simp_all [permMap, permMap', LoopWalk.map]
          <;>
          rfl
        rw [h₅] at h₄
        exact h₄
      refine' ⟨s_inv, h₁, _⟩
      <;> simp_all [permMapWalk]
      <;> rfl
    · -- Prove transitivity
      intro p q r h₁ h₂
      obtain ⟨s₁, h_eq₁, h_walk₁⟩ := h₁
      obtain ⟨s₂, h_eq₂, h_walk₂⟩ := h₂
      have s₃ : Equiv.Perm (Fin n) := s₂.comp s₁
      have h₃ : s₃ p.1 = r.1 := by
        calc
          s₃ p.1 = (s₂.comp s₁) p.1 := rfl
          _ = s₂ (s₁ p.1) := by simp [Equiv.comp_apply]
          _ = s₂ q.1 := by rw [h_eq₁]
          _ = r.1 := by rw [h_eq₂]
      have h₄ : h₃ ▸ (permMapWalk n s₃ p.2) = r.2 := by
        calc
          h₃ ▸ (permMapWalk n s₃ p.2) = h₃ ▸ (permMapWalk n (s₂.comp s₁) p.2) := rfl
          _ = h₃ ▸ (permMapWalk n s₂ (permMapWalk n s₁ p.2)) := by

          _ = r.2 := by

            have h₆ : h₃ ▸ (permMapWalk n s₂ (h_eq₁ ▸ q.2)) = r.2 := by

                _ = h₃ ▸ (permMapWalk n s₂ (h_eq₁ ▸ q.2)) := rfl

<;>
        simp_all [Equiv.inv_def]

      })
    · intro x y z hxy hyz
      -- We need to show that if x ~ y and y ~ z, then x ~ z.
      -- This is straightforward because the relation is transitive.
      simp_all [LoopWalkEquiv]
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try {
        obtain ⟨s, hs, h⟩ := hxy
        obtain ⟨t, ht, h'⟩ := hyz
        use s * t
        <;>
        simp_all [Equiv.mul_apply]
        <;>
        (try aesop)
      })

aesop
    · intro p q r
      unfold LoopWalkEquiv
      aesop
